window_note: 72h strict window (June 17-20) yielded 3 items; expanded to 5 days (June 15-20) per mandatory expansion rule.

items.title: headroom ships token-compression library and MCP server for AI agent workflows
key_claims: 60-95% token reduction (self-reported, unverified by independent benchmark)
92% reduction on code search: 17.7K to 1.4K tokens (self-reported)
65,694 to 5,118 tokens on SRE incident debug (self-reported)
38.7k GitHub stars, +12.8k week of June 17-20
caveats: Benchmarks self-reported in README. No independent reproduction. Token reduction is workload-dependent.

items.title: Claude Code v2.1.183 blocks destructive commands in auto mode
key_claims: git reset --hard, git checkout -- ., git clean -fd, git stash drop blocked unless explicitly requested
terraform destroy, pulumi destroy, cdk destroy blocked unless explicitly asked
git commit --amend blocked on commits not made by agent in current session
Deprecation warnings on stderr in print mode and agent frontmatter

items.title: WARP attack poisons deep-research AI agents via user-generated content
items.window_note: arXiv preprint May 22; media traction began June 15 in expanded window
key_claims: ~13-word injection in single Reddit post: 38-51% attack success rate
Multi-thread injection: up to 62% success rate
17-23% of deep-research agent retrieved content comes from user-generated sites
Gemini Deep Research cites UGC ~12%; OpenAI Deep Research ~0.4%
caveats: Commercial tool assessment based on citation behavior, not answer manipulation
Success rates vary by topic and platform

items.title: OpenAI updates GPT-5.5 Instant health performance; independent study documents safety gaps
items.primary_access_note: Primary URL returned HTTP 403; details confirmed via Search Engine Journal (T2, June 18)
key_claims: GPT-5.5 Instant performs at parity with OpenAI frontier Thinking models on HealthBench (vendor-claimed)
71% reduction in health responses flagged for factuality issues over 2 months (vendor-claimed, internal monitoring)
Physician panels rated GPT-5.5 Instant above physician-written responses in 3,500-response eval (vendor-claimed)
260 physicians across 60 countries in review network (vendor-claimed)
230 million weekly ChatGPT health queries (vendor-claimed)

conflicts.source: Nature Medicine (Ramaswamy et al., Mount Sinai, 2026-02)
conflicts.claim: 52% undertriage of gold-standard emergencies; 35% overtriage of non-urgent cases in ChatGPT Health prior version

caveats: All performance claims based on OpenAI internal benchmarks; no external audit
Nature Medicine study predates June 18 update; may not reflect current model
71% improvement is relative to GPT-5.3 Instant, not to medical-grade external standard

items.title: 150+ cybersecurity experts urge Commerce Department to reverse Fable 5 / Mythos 5 export ban
items.primary_access_note: Letter at freefable.org not directly fetched; Cybersecurity Dive and Fortune (both T2) used as sources
key_claims: Letter signed by Alex Stamos, Katie Moussouris, Rachel Tobac, Chris Wysopal, Joe Levy
76 signatures at publication (June 15); grew to 150+ by June 17
Addressed to Commerce Secretary Lutnick and National Cyber Director Cairncross
Argument: comparable capabilities available in GPT-5.5, Opus, Sonnet, Chinese models
caveats: Primary letter at freefable.org not fetched in this run
Signature count from secondary sources; may have grown further
No formal government response published as of June 20

items.title: Google Gemini Home Speaker opens pre-orders at $99.99, ships June 25
key_claims: Price: $99.99
Pre-order June 17; ship June 25
First Google smart speaker in six years
Thread 1.3 border-router support
Gemini Live and Camera History Search require Google Home Premium subscription
caveats: Advanced conversational features (Gemini Live) require paid subscription tier
US availability confirmed; international pricing not announced
